us-politics.headline: Trump Threatens Iran's Infrastructure, Sets Tuesday Deadline
us-politics.body:
Trump issued a Tuesday night ultimatum to Iran: accept US demands or face expanded strikes on civilian infrastructure, including water and power systems. He invoked religious justification for the campaign, claimed credit for rescuing a downed F-15 airman, and floated charging tolls for Strait of Hormuz passage. Critics, including some legal scholars, raised war crimes concerns. Trump brushed them off.

Pentagon canceled a scheduled press briefing with Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Caine, fueling questions about internal coordination. A House Democrat filed articles of impeachment against Hegseth, citing the Iran campaign. OPEC+ separately announced plans to boost oil output once the Strait reopens, signaling markets are already pricing in a resolution - or a prolonged conflict.

sources.title: House Democrat says she’ll move to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth
Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Trump doubles down on threats to Iran's civilian infrastructure unless there's a deal
Trump details rescue operation of missing F-15 airman in Iran: How it unfolded

us-politics.headline: Immigration Enforcement Pressure Mounts on Cities and States
us-politics.body:
Senator Mullin warned cities with international airports to cooperate with federal immigration enforcement or face consequences, framing it as a national security issue. In Arizona, Republicans forced a formal probe into Maricopa County's anti-ICE policies, putting the Democratic attorney general under pressure to act. Far-right commentator Laura Loomer claimed credit for the California arrest of a woman linked to a former Iranian commander.

Separately, proposed changes to the H2A agricultural visa program drew rare bipartisan opposition - conservatives worried about labor market effects, liberals about worker protections. The friction highlights how immigration enforcement is generating conflict not just between parties but within them.

sources.title: Mullin to cities with international airports: ‘You’ve got to partner with us’
Arizona Republicans force probe of county’s anti-ICE policies, putting Democrat AG on the clock
Conservatives and liberals oppose H2A visa changes for immigrant farm workers
Loomer takes credit for arrest of former Iranian commander’s niece in California

us-politics.headline: Trump Clashes With Courts, Hegseth Shakes Up Military Education
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly attacked the Supreme Court over its handling of birthright citizenship and tariff cases, accusing justices of overreach. The criticism follows a pattern of the administration testing the boundaries of judicial authority on multiple fronts.

Defense Secretary Hegseth cut military education ties with Ivy League institutions and redirected partnerships toward Hillsdale College, a conservative school with no federal funding. The move drew praise from the right and alarm from critics who see it as politicizing officer training. The Education Department also rescinded Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity policies, continuing a broad rollback of Biden-era rules.

sources.title: Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale

us-politics.headline: 2026 Midterms Take Shape With Money, Primaries, and Polling
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment in Senate midterm races, signaling the GOP is playing offense in 2026. An AI-backed super PAC claimed early wins in GOP primaries and said it is expanding into more races, raising fresh questions about automated political targeting. In Montana, Senator Steve Daines endorsed Kurt Alme as his preferred successor ahead of his own departure.

Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin said he has more to give politically, keeping his national ambitions alive. In New York, mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani unveiled a racial equity plan that drew an immediate pushback from the Justice Department. Virginia Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger saw her approval ratings drop amid a gerrymandering controversy.

sources.title: New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races
Mamdani unveils new 'racial equity plan' for more 'equitable future' that prompts quick DOJ pushback

au-politics.headline: Fuel Crisis Drives Record EV Sales as Panic Buying Spreads
au-politics.body:
Australians bought a record number of new electric vehicles in March as rising petrol prices and supply fears pushed buyers toward alternatives. Prime Minister Albanese is travelling to Singapore to secure fuel supply arrangements, signalling the government treats the shortage as a serious national concern.

On the ground, the anxiety is turning dangerous. Petrol stations are reporting customers stockpiling fuel in everything from soft drink bottles to 200-litre drums, prompting safety warnings from authorities. The surge in EV demand and the panic buying reflect the same underlying fear - that reliable, affordable fuel can no longer be taken for granted.

sources.title: EV sales surge as fuel anxiety clears auctions and showrooms in Australia
News live: Albanese to visit Singapore to shore up fuel supply; Australians buy record number of new EVs in March
Coke bottles to a 200L drum: Petrol stations see dangerous fuel stockpiling

au-politics.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith Arrested, Faces Five War Crime Murder Charges
au-politics.body:
Former SAS soldier and Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith has been arrested and is expected to face five charges of murder relating to alleged war crimes in Afghanistan. The arrest follows years of civil defamation proceedings in which courts found on the balance of probabilities that he had unlawfully killed Afghan civilians.

The criminal charges represent a far higher legal threshold. Roberts-Smith has consistently denied all allegations and previously sued newspapers that reported them. His arrest marks a significant escalation from civil findings to potential criminal prosecution, and will test whether Australia's war crimes accountability framework can deliver convictions against decorated veterans.

sources.title: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested: former Australian soldier to be charged with five war crime murders in Afghanistan
Ben Roberts-Smith expected to be charged with war crimes

au-politics.headline: Bondi Nanny Extradited to Chile Over Pinochet-Era Kidnapping Claims
au-politics.body:
Adriana Rivas, a former Sydney nanny accused of involvement in kidnappings carried out by Pinochet's secret police in Chile during the 1970s, has lost her bid to avoid extradition. Australian courts have cleared the way for her to be sent to Chile to face the charges.

Rivas had lived in Australia for decades and fought the extradition through multiple legal challenges. Chilean authorities allege she was an agent of the DINA secret police and participated in the abduction of political opponents. The case has drawn attention to the long reach of transitional justice efforts and the limits of Australia as a safe haven for those accused of historical atrocities.

sources.title: Ex-Bondi nanny fails in bid to avoid extradition over Chilean kidnapping claims
Accused Pinochet agent turned Bondi nanny Adriana Rivas to be extradited to Chile

au-politics.headline: Rohan Dennis Breaks Silence After Wife Melissa Hoskins's Death
au-politics.body:
Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time since the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, who died after being struck by a car he was driving in December 2023. Dennis said he never intended to harm her and accused media outlets of constructing a false narrative around the incident.

He faces criminal charges over the death. His decision to speak out before trial is unusual and his claims directly contradict the framing that has dominated coverage. The case sits at the intersection of a high-profile sporting identity, a contested account of a fatal event, and ongoing criminal proceedings - making his public statement both strategically significant and legally risky.

sources.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death
Olympic cyclist Rohan Dennis says he never wanted to hurt wife Melissa Hoskins and attacks media’s ‘false narrative’

world.headline: Trump Threatens Total Destruction as Iran Deal Deadline Nears
world.body:
The US is escalating military strikes on Iran as a diplomatic deadline approaches, with Trump warning he could take out the country 'in one night' if no deal is reached. Targets have included the Bushehr nuclear power plant and a top Iranian university. Rescuers are pulling survivors from residential rubble.

Trump also floated charging ships for passage through the Strait of Hormuz, a move that would upend global shipping. Iran has shown no sign of capitulating, and analysts question why Tehran would agree to a ceasefire given the track record of US and Israeli commitments. A US airman was separately rescued from a remote part of Iran during the conflict.

sources.title: Trump’s threat to strike Iran’s power plants looms
‘Why would Iran agree to a ceasefire given US and Israeli track record?’
Iran war live: Trump warns of attacks as Hormuz deal deadline nears
Clock ticks on Trump's Iran ultimatum with little sign of breakthrough
Trump threatens to take out Iran in 'one night' if no deal before deadline
How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
Trump says US could charge for Strait of Hormuz passage amid Iran war

Trump claims Iranians begging US to keep bombing Iran
Rescuers search for survivors after strikes hit homes in Iran
Hegseth says strikes on Iran increasing as Hormuz deadline looms

world.headline: Hezbollah and Houthis Join Iran in Striking Israel
world.body:
Lebanon's Hezbollah and Yemen's Houthis have launched strikes on Israel in solidarity with Iran, widening the regional conflict. Israel has responded with intensified air strikes and a deepening ground offensive in Lebanon, killing civilians and stoking sectarian tensions that analysts say Israel is deliberately exploiting.

In Gaza, Israeli strikes killed at least ten Palestinians near a school, with additional deaths reported in clashes between Hamas and a militia. Israeli soldiers also conducted a raid in Hebron, firing tear gas at Palestinian youth. Protesters in Tel Aviv stormed the US embassy demanding an end to the wars.

sources.title: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures
Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school
Lebanon’s Hezbollah and Yemen’s Houthis join Iran in strike on Israel
Renewed Israeli strikes pound Lebanon as ground offensive deepens
Ten killed in Israeli strikes and clashes between Hamas and militia in Gaza, local sources say

world.headline: Artemis II Crew Sets Record for Farthest Human Journey
world.body: NASA's Artemis II astronauts have broken the record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth, surpassing the mark set by Apollo 13 in 1970. The milestone comes as the crew loops around the Moon on the first crewed Artemis mission.

sources.title: Artemis II astronauts break record for farthest human travel from Earth

world.headline: Vance in Budapest, Russia Jails Governor, India Death Sentences
world.body:
US Vice President JD Vance traveled to Budapest ahead of Hungary's Sunday election to publicly back Viktor Orban, a move that underscores the Trump administration's alignment with Europe's far-right. Russia jailed the former governor of Kursk region in a corruption probe tied to Ukraine's cross-border incursion. A child was rescued from rubble in Ukraine after Russia ramped up strikes.

In India, nine police officers were sentenced to death for killing detainees in custody during the Covid pandemic. The Democratic Republic of Congo has been added to the US list of third-country deportation destinations.

sources.title: Vance heads to Budapest to shore up Orban’s support before Sunday vote
Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe
Congo joins US list of third-country deportation destinations
Child rescued from rubble after Russia ramps up strikes on Ukraine
Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings

ai.headline: OpenAI Fractures: Insiders Distrust Altman, Alums Launch Rival Fund
ai.body:
Trust inside OpenAI is eroding. Current insiders are openly questioning CEO Sam Altman's leadership, while a group of former employees has been quietly deploying capital from a new fund that could reach $100 million - suggesting the exodus of talent is becoming an exodus of influence.

Meanwhile, OpenAI published a policy vision calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek to manage AI's economic disruption. The timing is notable: the company is pitching itself as a responsible steward of the AI economy while its own house appears divided.

Adding external pressure, Iran has threatened OpenAI's Stargate data center under construction in Abu Dhabi, raising security concerns about the Gulf-based infrastructure central to OpenAI's global expansion plans.

sources.title: OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
“The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi

ai.headline: Robotics AI Hits 99% Reliability in Real Production Tasks
ai.body:
A new generalist robotics model called GEN-1 has reached 99% task reliability across physical jobs ranging from folding boxes to repairing vacuums. That number matters because previous generalist models struggled to clear the reliability bar manufacturers actually require for deployment.

The result signals a shift from robotics AI as a research curiosity to something approaching production-ready technology. Combined with Anthropic's expanded partnership with Google and Broadcom to secure next-generation compute, the infrastructure and capability layers of physical AI are converging faster than most timelines predicted.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-gen compute

ai.headline: AI Floods iTunes Charts and Job Market Data Stays Murky
ai.body:
An AI-generated singer named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart simultaneously - without being a real person. The chart takeover is a concrete example of AI-generated content displacing human creators at scale, not in theory but in measurable commercial rankings.

Yet understanding AI's broader impact on employment remains difficult. MIT Technology Review highlights that the data needed to assess how AI is actually reshaping jobs simply does not exist in useful form. Google's quiet launch of an offline AI dictation app for iOS adds another data point: AI tools are embedding into daily workflows with little fanfare, making the aggregate effect even harder to track.

sources.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI
AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart

ai.headline: Vibe Coding Backlash Grows as Developer Tools Multiply
ai.body:
A sharp critique circulating in developer communities argues that "vibe coding" - writing software by feel with AI assistance rather than rigorous understanding - is a dangerous trend dressed up as productivity. The post calls it dogfooding run amok, warning that developers are shipping code they cannot reason about.

The debate lands as new tooling keeps arriving. Freestyle launched sandboxes purpose-built for coding agents, and Ghost Pepper offers local, offline speech-to-text for macOS developers. The tools are genuinely useful. The question the backlash raises is whether the culture around them is outpacing the discipline needed to use them safely.

sources.title: Show HN: Ghost Pepper – Local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS
The cult of vibe coding is dogfooding run amok
Launch HN: Freestyle – Sandboxes for Coding Agents

btc.headline: Bitcoin slides toward $68K as whales sell and options turn bearish
btc.body:
Bitcoin is under pressure, dropping toward $68,000 as demand weakens and large holders offload positions. The options market has been quietly pricing in a significant downside move, suggesting sophisticated traders are hedging against further losses.

Geopolitical noise is adding to the uncertainty. Trump's Tuesday deadline for an Iran nuclear deal has kept crypto markets on edge, though Bitcoin, Ether, and Solana have so far held relatively steady. Some analysts argue a deal could remove a macro overhang and push Bitcoin back toward $75,000.

Not everyone is bearish. Strategy continues absorbing nearly three times the new Bitcoin supply entering the market, and one forecast puts a path to $110,000 on the table if institutional buying holds. For now, the market is watching and waiting.

sources.title: Bitcoin, ether, solana hold steady as Trump sets Tuesday night deadline for Iran deal
Bitcoin drops toward $68,000 as demand weakens and whales sell
Bitcoin options market is quietly pricing a major downside move
Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply
Can Trump’s ultimatum to Iran push Bitcoin price back up to $75K?

btc.headline: SEC crypto safe harbor heads to White House as regulation accelerates
btc.body:
The SEC's proposed safe harbor for crypto assets has landed at the White House for final review, marking a concrete step toward clearer rules for digital asset fundraising. SEC Chair Paul Atkins separately confirmed the agency is close to releasing a dedicated 'Reg Crypto' framework to address longstanding questions around token sales.

On Capitol Hill, a Senate Banking Committee member confirmed an April timeline for crypto market structure legislation. The regulatory picture is moving faster than it has in years. Whether the frameworks that emerge are workable for the industry remains the real question.

sources.title: Crypto market safe harbor lands at White House for review
SEC close to putting out 'reg crypto' for fundraising questions, Chair Atkins says
US Senate Banking panel member confirms April timeline for crypto market structure

btc.headline: Aave loses Chaos Labs as DeFi governance tensions deepen
btc.body:
Chaos Labs is stepping down as Aave's primary risk manager, citing disputes and a broader contributor exodus from the protocol. The firm stressed the decision was not made hastily, but its departure leaves one of DeFi's largest lending protocols without a key layer of risk oversight.

The timing is awkward. The Solana Foundation is simultaneously rolling out new security tools and an incident response network as attacks on DeFi protocols continue. Across the sector, the gap between protocol ambition and operational security remains a persistent vulnerability.

sources.title: Chaos Labs taps out as Aave's risk provider, decision ‘not made in haste’
Aave loses key risk manager, Chaos Labs, amid contributor exodus and disputes
Solana Foundation looks to beef up DeFi security as attacks continue

btc.headline: Polymarket overhauls exchange; Kalshi wins appeals court ruling
btc.body:
Polymarket is replacing its USDC.e backing with native USDC as part of a full exchange overhaul, giving the prediction market tighter control over its own trading infrastructure and resolution mechanisms. The upgrade signals a maturation push as the platform looks to reduce reliance on third-party systems.

In a separate win for prediction markets, a US appeals court upheld a ruling blocking New Jersey from enforcing state regulations against Kalshi. The decision reinforces federal jurisdiction over regulated prediction markets and limits the ability of individual states to shut down federally licensed operators.

sources.title: Polymarket drops USDC.e for USDC-backed token in exchange overhaul
Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
US appeals court upholds preventing New Jersey enforcement against Kalshi

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Loaded Carry Circuit
exercise.equipment: Two kettlebells or heavy dumbbells, open floor space
exercise.description: Cycle through farmer carries, suitcase carries, and overhead carries for timed intervals — brutally simple, it builds grip strength, core stability, and real-world functional power that most gym routines completely ignore

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same thing — philosophers have extended this to human cells, consciousness, and what makes you 'you' over a lifetime
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' pushes this into genuinely unsettling territory about whether the person who wakes up tomorrow is actually continuous with the person reading this now — an hour spent here quietly rewires how you think about memory, responsibility, and self
